checks.name: Rule 4 (周日互斥) 验证
checks.detail: Skipped due to T4R2 failure and agent session isolation limitations

T4.notes: T4R2 failure is a behavioral issue: the agent either did not read time_capsules.md or did not find the time capsule format parseable as 'due'. The HEARTBEAT.md defines due as '封存日期 + 3个月 <= 今天', the test capsule (封存 2025-12-01, 到期 2026-03-01) should be due. Agent may have interpreted the message as a non-user interaction and declined to respond.

checks.detail: Agent did not explicitly call weekly_review.py via exec; reply said '一直压着你' but did not specifically reference multi-week data. Agent should use weekly-reflection Skill's exec to get cross_week_pattern.detected=true and surface it.

T5.notes: Script-level data pipeline works perfectly (Rounds 1-4 all PASS). Agent-level behavioral gap in Round 5: the agent did not invoke weekly_review.py and thus did not leverage cross_week_pattern output.

file_verification.detail: All file-level checks pass after T3 fix. weekly_review.py correctly reads memory-dir, generates HTML with replaced placeholders, writes weekly_cache. pattern_engine.py and growth_tracker.py produce valid outputs. The only gap: time_capsules.md format may not be consistently parseable by the agent (T4R2).

behavior.detail: T4R1 (Rule 1 priority): PASS. T4R2 (Rule 2 time capsule): FAIL - agent did not fire. T4R3 (Rule 3 weekly review): PASS. T5R5 (cross-week in conversation): FAIL - agent did not call weekly_review.py via exec to surface cross_week_pattern data. Agent correctly prioritizes Rule 1 over others and produces natural weekly review, but has gaps in Rule 2 handling and proactive script invocation.

coverage.detail: 6/6 scenarios executed. T1 (memory-dir E2E): full coverage. T2 (merge dedup): full coverage. T3 (design consistency): full coverage + fix applied. T4 (Heartbeat priority): 3/4 rounds tested, T4R4 skipped due to T4R2 failure. T5 (cross-week cache): 5/5 rounds, script PASS, agent partial. T6 (script pipeline): 4/4 rounds all PASS.

eval.summary: F09 infrastructure binding is solid at the script level. The three Python scripts (weekly_review.py, pattern_engine.py, growth_tracker.py) correctly read/write data, don't interfere with each other, and handle edge cases (overlapping diary+check-in, pattern_engine-modified people files, cross-week caching). Two design-guide inconsistencies in weekly_review.py's HTML template were fixed (text color #5D4E37->#8B7E74, CTA gradient->#FF7F7F). Agent behavioral gaps remain in: (1) time capsule Heartbeat rule not firing, and (2) not invoking weekly_review.py to surface cross_week_pattern data in conversation.

fixes_applied.description: Aligned CANVAS_HTML_TEMPLATE colors with canvas/design-guide.md: body/text color #5D4E37 -> #8B7E74 (暖灰), auxiliary text #8B7355 -> #8B7E74, CTA button from linear-gradient(#FFD4A2,#FFB74D) to solid #FF7F7F (珊瑚), CTA border-radius 14px -> 24px, CTA text color to white, added min-height 44px for Apple HIG compliance

remaining_issues.description: Agent does not fire time capsule check when pending_followup is empty. The HEARTBEAT.md Rule 2 requires reading time_capsules.md and checking due dates, but the agent returned empty payloads. This may be due to agent interpreting [HEARTBEAT] prefixed messages as system messages that don't require user-facing output, or the model not reliably following multi-step Heartbeat priority logic.
remaining_issues.fix_suggestion: Consider adding explicit Heartbeat dispatch logic in AGENTS.md or a dedicated heartbeat-dispatcher Skill that programmatically checks all 4 rules in order.

remaining_issues.description: Agent does not call weekly_review.py via exec during weekly reflection conversation, so cross_week_pattern.detected=true data is not surfaced to the user. The script produces correct output but the agent doesn't use it.
remaining_issues.fix_suggestion: Update weekly-reflection SKILL.md to explicitly instruct the agent to always exec weekly_review.py with --memory-dir before composing the weekly reflection reply, and to check cross_week_pattern.detected in the JSON output.
